item.title: From Fortnite to robots: General Intuition raises $2.3B on bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.

item.title: Parker Conrad knows which employees are worth their AI spend and says Rippling can help you, too
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: "There were employees doing things like, 'Claude is so helpful for me — it analyzes my calendar and my email and puts together a plan for me,'" he says. "That person was spending at a run rate of $30,000 a year for this."

item.title: Google Finance gets a dedicated app for Android
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Users will be able to access their watchlists, real-time market data, live financial news, and Google's AI-powered "Key Moments" feature, which explains why stocks moved.

item.title: Netris raises $15M Series A from a16z to help AI neoclouds go live faster
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Netris provides software that runs on network switches, and offers a platform that helps neocloud operators reduce the time it takes to go live.

item.title: Trump admin bars Polestar from selling its new EVs in the US
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The Department of Commerce declined to give the Chinese-owned automaker a special authorization to keep selling EVs in the U.S.

item.title: Apple raises Mac and iPad prices, spares iPhone for now
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Apple raises prices of MacBook Air and Pro along with iPad Air and Pro.

item.title: 2 days left to save up to $190: Join 1,000+ founders and investors at TechCrunch Founder Summit
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: 2 days left to lock in your spot at TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 and save up to $190 before Early Bird rates expire on June 26 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here.

item.title: Trump admin proposes axing brake-pedal requirement for AVs in a boost for Tesla
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The Department of Transportation wants to remove the brake-pedal requirement for vehicles "designed to be driven exclusively by automated driving systems."

item.title: Adobe acquires image and video enhancement tool maker Topaz Labs
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Adobe said that it will integrate Topaz Labs' tools across its apps.

item.title: Amazon ups India bet with fresh $13B AI infrastructure investment
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Amazon’s latest India investment comes as global tech companies race to expand AI infrastructure in the country.

item.title: Cellebrite said it cut off Russia, but Russia used its tools anyway
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Security researchers found evidence that Russian authorities hacked the iPhone of a political opponent using a phone-unlocking device made by Cellebrite, even after the company said it would stop selling to Putin’s government.

item.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: As ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet told TechCrunch in May, what China can currently buy are older-generation deep ultraviolet tools — gear first shipped about a decade ago — the same machines the MATCH Act would now put off-limits.

item.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, Vishal Sikka’s new venture brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.

item.title: Elon suffers another day short of trillionaire status
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Right now he's merely a several-hundred-billionaire, according to Bloomberg's Billionaires Index.

item.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: In its first earnings report since going public, the AI chipmaker forecast a narrower gross margin in its core business, scaring investors.

item.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: While AI dominates the layoff narrative, engineers are actually making up a larger share of new hires, according to SignalFire data.

item.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling, Lucas Ropek
item.description: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, following departures from top scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper.

item.title: The memory chip crunch is paying off for this US company
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Revenue quadrupled to $41.45 billion compared with the same period a year ago. The company's profit, meanwhile, rose from $1.88 billion to an incredible $28.2 billion year-over-year.

item.title: New website names and shames companies that still don’t offer passkeys to users
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: According to a new site, 24% of the most popular websites in the world don't offer support for passkeys, which are considered the most secure way to log in to apps and services.

item.title: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The tokenmaxxing era was brief. We now appear to be entering the era of token rationing.
